skill_reading_music.title: Reading Music
skill_reading_music.description: The ability to accurately read, internalize, and perform sheet music in real-time.

levels.title: Basic Clefs & Rhythms
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can confidently identify notes residing strictly within the five lines of either the Treble or Bass clef. They can maintain a steady pulse while performing whole, half, and quarter note rhythms.

levels.title: The Grand Staff & Simple Meter
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can transition fluidly between the treble and bass clefs across the grand staff. They understand fundamental dynamics (forte, piano) and perform eighth-note subdivisions cleanly within 4/4 and 3/4 time.

levels.title: Expanded Range & Syncopation
levels.masteryCriteria: The student effortlessly reads outside the standard staves up to two ledger lines. They handle accidental alterations (sharps and flats) on the fly, and can perform syncopated figures involving dotted notes and precise 16th note groupings.

levels.title: Advanced Meter
levels.masteryCriteria: The student naturally feels the macro-beat in compound meters like 6/8 and 9/8, seamlessly differentiating between triplet stress patterns and straight eighth groupings.

skill_music_theory.title: Music Theory
skill_music_theory.description: The structural understanding of harmony, scales, intervals, and tonality.

levels.title: The Foundation of Tonality
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can quickly determine the numeric distance between any two notes on a staff and understands the specific whole/half step formula required to construct a standard Major scale.

levels.title: Keys & Diatonic Harmony
levels.masteryCriteria: The student can instantly identify a major key from any sharp or flat key signature. Given a root note, they can rapidly stack thirds to construct flawless Major and minor triads.

levels.title: Advanced Tonality & Voicing
levels.masteryCriteria: The student understands the concept of relative minor keys and can deduce them from a Major key signature. They can rapidly identify basic triads regardless of whether the root, third, or fifth is in the bass voice.

metadata.title: First Steps: Intro to Music
metadata.description: The absolute basics of how music is written. Learn what a staff is, how notes go up and down, and the fundamental concept of a steady beat.
metadata.learningOutcome: Understands the basic grid of written music (pitch vs time). Can identify C4, D4 and E4 on a G clef staff.

metadata.title: Basic Rhythm & Rests
metadata.description: Learn the most common note durations: whole, half, quarter, and their corresponding rests.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can clap or tap basic rhythmic patterns.

metadata.title: Reading the Treble Clef
metadata.description: Learn the letter names of the lines and spaces specifically for the Treble Clef (G Clef).
metadata.learningOutcome: Can fluently name notes from E4 to F5.

metadata.title: Reading the Bass Clef
metadata.description: Learn the letter names of the lines and spaces for the Bass Clef (F Clef).
metadata.learningOutcome: Can fluently name notes from G2 to A3.

metadata.title: The Grand Staff & Middle C
metadata.description: See how the Treble and Bass clefs connect to form the Grand Staff, bridged by Middle C.
metadata.learningOutcome: Understands the continuous relationship of pitches across both clefs.

metadata.title: Eighth Notes & Subdivisions
metadata.description: Break the beat in half. Learn to read and perform pairs of eighth notes and eighth rests.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can accurately count and perform '1 and 2 and' rhythms.

metadata.title: Simple Time Signatures
metadata.description: Learn how music is grouped into measures. Covers 4/4 (Common Time), 3/4, and 2/4.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can identify the meter of a piece and place bar lines correctly.

metadata.title: Ledger Lines: Just Outside the Staff
metadata.description: Learn to read notes up to two ledger lines above and below the Treble and Bass clefs.
metadata.learningOutcome: Comfortably reads an extended range suitable for intermediate sheet music.

metadata.title: Sharps, Flats, & Naturals
metadata.description: Introduction to chromatic alteration using accidental symbols.
metadata.learningOutcome: Understands how to raise or lower a pitch by a half-step.

metadata.title: The C Major Scale
metadata.description: The foundation of Western harmony. Learn the specific pattern of whole and half steps that builds a Major scale.
metadata.learningOutcome: Knows the formula W-W-H-W-W-W-H and can construct C Major.

metadata.title: Basic Intervals (Numeric)
metadata.description: Learn to instantly recognize the distance between two notes on the staff (2nds, 3rds, 4ths, 5ths, etc.).
metadata.learningOutcome: Can visually identify the numeric size of an interval without counting lines.

metadata.title: Sharp Key Signatures
metadata.description: Learn the order of sharps and how to identify major keys from G to B.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can instantly name a major key by looking at its sharp signature.

metadata.title: Flat Key Signatures
metadata.description: Learn the order of flats and how to identify major keys from F to Db.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can instantly name a major key by looking at its flat signature.

metadata.title: Relative Minor Keys
metadata.description: Discover the 'sad' sibling of every major key. Learn how to find the relative minor.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can identify both the Major and Minor key a signature represents.

metadata.title: Dotted Rhythms
metadata.description: Learn how the dot alters a note's length. Focus on dotted half notes and the dotted-quarter/eighth pair.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can accurately perform syncopated and dotted rhythmic figures.

metadata.title: Compound Time (6/8, 9/8)
metadata.description: Explore meters where the beat naturally divides into threes instead of twos.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can feel and count music in compound meter without confusing it for triplets.

metadata.title: Major & Minor Triads
metadata.description: Stacking thirds to build basic chords. Learn the difference between Major and minor triads.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can construct and identify root-position Major and minor triads.

metadata.title: Chord Inversions
metadata.description: Learn what happens when the root isn't on the bottom. Covers 1st and 2nd inversions.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can identify standard triads regardless of voicing order.

metadata.title: 16th Notes & Faster Rhythms
metadata.description: Breaking beats into quarters. Learn how to read grouping of four 16th notes.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can count '1 e & a' fluidly.

metadata.title: Dynamics & Articulation
metadata.description: How loud? How short? Learn the symbols for piano, forte, staccato, accents, and slurs.
metadata.learningOutcome: Can interpret stylistic markings to play with expression.
